item.title: Security scanner alert: potential real secrets in git history — sk-ant-, ghp_, AKIA, private keys
item.intent: Git history may contain real API keys and tokens. If any are live, they must be rotated immediately and history scrubbed. False positives must be confirmed as false.
item.description: Automated security scanner flagged 131x sk-ant- matches, 7x ghp_ (GitHub PATs), 16x AKIA (AWS keys), 5x sk- patterns, 1x Slack token, 6x private key headers in clawhq git history. Also 1x sk-ant- each in simonplant-com, simonplant.com, easy-markdown, and 2x in toolkit. Most are likely false positives from test fixtures (scanner.test.ts, probes.test.ts, patterns.ts) but git history matches need human review. Ref: GitHub issue #5.
steps: Run git log -p --all -S 'sk-ant-' to find actual commits containing Anthropic key patterns — distinguish test fixtures from real keys
Run git log -p --all -S 'ghp_' to find GitHub PAT commits — check if tokens are real (40+ hex chars after prefix)
Run git log -p --all -S 'AKIA' to find AWS key commits — check for real 20-char key IDs
Run git log -p --all -S 'PRIVATE KEY' to find private key header commits
For any confirmed real secrets: rotate immediately (GitHub Settings, AWS Console, Anthropic Console)
Use git filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner to scrub confirmed secrets from history
Verify scrub succeeded: re-run scanner, confirm zero real matches
Close GitHub issue #5 with findings summary
acceptanceCriteria: All git history matches reviewed and classified as real or false positive
Any confirmed real secrets rotated at the source (API dashboard, console)
Git history scrubbed of confirmed secrets using git filter-repo or BFG
Re-scan confirms zero real secret matches in git history
GitHub issue #5 closed with summary of findings and actions taken
